title: The Guarded Gateway
subtitle: The twelve evangelical counsels Christ added to the commandments

stillness: Be still, and consider the land of your own soul.
Its whole strength lies in its gateway.
Let the noise within you grow quiet, and keep watch there.
gospel: go, sell whatever you have, and give to the poor,
Whoever does not renounce all that he possesses
If anyone would come after me, let him deny himself.
If anyone strike you on the right cheek,
Be not anxious. Do not think about tomorrow.
Let your speech be: yes, yes; no, no.
behold: We have spoken of the commandments, which are necessary for salvation;
now consider the counsels, which are required for perfection.
Sometimes the strength of a land stands in its entrance,
and that passage needs good guarding;
for once the entrance is won, the enemy easily takes the rest.
So it is in the land of the soul:
its whole strength stands in the entrance — in the occasions of sin —
and for this a good watch is needed about it.
For the guarding of this entrance the counsels are given,
which contain and keep the commandments,
and draw a man back from heedless glances, from wanderings, and from other perils.
There are twelve evangelical counsels, which Christ added to the precepts.
consider: Every evil rises from a threefold root:
from the concupiscence of the flesh,
from the concupiscence of the eyes,
and from the pride of life.
Poverty draws you far from the concupiscence of the eyes;
chastity, from the concupiscence of the flesh;
obedience, from the pride of life.
These three estrange their keepers from evil —
not only from the guilt, but even from the cause.
To wish your enemy grace and glory is necessity;
but to pour out works of kindness upon him, and show him signs of goodwill,
this belongs to the counsel and to perfection.
To give what is superfluous in another's extreme need is duty;
but to give the very things you yourself have need of — this is the counsel.
Not only sins, but the occasions of sin are to be fled.
They bind heavy burdens upon men, but say and do not.
Cast all your anxiety upon Him.
These precepts and counsels are all easy to one who wills,
and who holds the fear of God before his eyes.
prayer: Let the fear of God be to you more forceful than any necessity.
For if you are always willing to set the occasions before yourself,
you will keep nothing of the things commanded,
and so you will trample underfoot all that is enjoined.
Lord Jesus, set this holy fear before my eyes,
guard the gateway of my soul,
and make your counsels easy to me who wills to follow you.

latinJewel.latin: Sic est in terra animæ, quia tota fortitudo ejus consistit in ingressu, scilicet in occasionibus peccatorum, et propter hoc necessaria est bona custodia circa ipsum.
latinJewel.english: So it is in the land of the soul: for its whole strength stands in the entrance — that is, in the occasions of sins — and for this a good watch about it is necessary.
